steps.title: Find the main clause
steps.content: The sentence first gives a complete main clause: Antonio Martorell's 2010 woodcut print La Playa Negra I (Tar Beach I) has been displayed at the Smithsonian American Art Museum. The words after the blank, which features a large collection of contemporary art, add extra information about the museum. That kind of nonessential which clause needs a comma before it, not a new independent-clause boundary.

steps.title: Test the wrong boundaries
steps.content: Choice A, Museum and which, uses and which without a parallel earlier which clause. Choice B, Museum; which, puts a semicolon before a relative clause, but a which clause is not independent. Choice C, Museum. Which, turns the relative clause into a sentence fragment.

steps.title: Attach the extra information
steps.content: Choice D, Museum, which, keeps the museum in the main clause and attaches the nonessential relative clause with the required comma. The completed sentence says that the work has been displayed at the Smithsonian American Art Museum, which features a large collection of contemporary art. The answer is D.
